checks.expectation: At least one of CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, or .cursor/rules/*.mdc exists at the repository root.
checks.gap: None. Both AGENTS.md (agents.md convention) and CLAUDE.md exist at the root, with a deliberate primary/secondary split.

checks.expectation: The primary file is at least 25 lines and has structure beyond a single bullet list.
checks.gap: None. AGENTS.md is 59 lines with headed sections (What this repo is, Run it, Golden rules, Where to look, Safety) plus a routing table.

checks.expectation: The primary file is at most 400 lines; instruction files must not crowd out the user's problem in context.
checks.gap: None. Both root files are deliberately thin (59 and 47 lines) and delegate detail to .agents/, the documented single-source-of-truth pattern.
checks.remediation: No action. This thin-entrypoint + .agents/ detail split is a model design.

checks.expectation: The instruction file states what the project is and who it is for in identifiable prose.
checks.gap: None. AGENTS.md 'What this repo is' states the purpose (a reference scaffold for agentic engineering; TypeScript+React is the runnable carrier). CLAUDE.md restates the two showcases.

checks.expectation: Names the primary framework, language, and runtime.
checks.gap: None. TECH_STACK.md gives a one-owner-per-concern table (TypeScript, React 19, Vite, Jest+RTL, Playwright, Biome) and Node >=20 is in AGENTS.md and package.json engines.

checks.expectation: A brief architectural orientation: directory layout, dominant module pattern, or links to ARCHITECTURE.md.
checks.gap: None. ARCHITECTURE.md documents the feature-folder vertical-slice pattern with a directory tree, the view/hook/pure-logic split, and the index.ts public-surface rule. Both root files route to it.

checks.expectation: Domain glossary present where domain terminology is used; canonical human-facing docs are linked.
checks.gap: None. GLOSSARY.md defines the ubiquitous language (Feature, Public surface, Behaviour map, Quality gate, StyleProof modes). AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/.agents/README.md cross-link README, ARCHITECTURE.md, ADRs, templates. No CONTRIBUTING.md exists, so its absence from links is not a gap.

checks.expectation: Lists canonical install, dev-server, test, and build commands.
checks.gap: None. AGENTS.md 'Run it' block documents pnpm install / setup:agents / dev / verify / e2e; DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md and both skills add typecheck, check, fallow:audit, test:coverage. All resolve to real package.json scripts (verified in Layer 4).

checks.expectation: States naming patterns, import-ordering, formatting, abbreviations policy.
checks.gap: None. CONVENTIONS.md is concrete and mechanical: kebab-case folders, PascalCase components, useCamelCase hooks, SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE config constants, no-abbreviations rule, import-type rule, Biome-owned import ordering.

checks.expectation: States the commit-message convention and any git add policy.
checks.gap: None. Conventional Commits is documented and enforced on commit-msg via commitlint. Subject form is specified (imperative mood, lower case, no trailing period). Branch-naming is not stated, but the commit convention is mechanically enforced.
checks.remediation: Optional: add a one-line branch-naming convention to CONVENTIONS.md for completeness.

checks.expectation: States the testing convention: framework, query priority, behaviour-over-implementation, snapshot policy.
checks.gap: None. Tests exist; the convention is documented in depth: Jest + RTL behaviour-first with getByRole priority, userEvent over fireEvent, Playwright behaviour map as the contract, 80% coverage threshold, pure-logic-vs-hook test placement.

checks.expectation: Explicit agent guidance with both positive ('prefer') and negative ('avoid') items.
checks.gap: None. AGENTS.md golden rules + Safety provide both: positive (read .agents first, copy templates, propose ADRs) and negative (never bypass gates / --no-verify, no second linter/test runner, do not weaken tsconfig/biome, never edit generated artefacts, do not commit secrets).

checks.expectation: settings.json parses as JSON with no syntax errors.
checks.gap: None. Parses cleanly; also pins a $schema for editor validation.

checks.expectation: .gitignore (root or .claude/.gitignore) excludes settings.local.json so personal overrides never leak into the shared project.
checks.gap: Neither the root .gitignore nor a .claude/.gitignore excludes settings.local.json. No .claude/ pattern is present. settings.local.json does not exist yet, so nothing has leaked — but the moment a contributor adds personal overrides (a local model pin, extra allow patterns, an env value) the file is committable. For a template repo this is amplified: every consumer inherits the gap.
checks.remediation: Add `.claude/settings.local.json` to the root .gitignore (the file already has a 'Local env + editor noise' section that is the natural home). One line; zero blast radius.

checks.expectation: settings.json contains no secret-shaped values or secret-named keys with concrete values.
checks.gap: None. The file holds only permission patterns and a hook command; no env block, no apiKey/token/secret/password keys, no sk-/ghp-/xoxb-/JWT/AWS-shaped strings.

checks.expectation: permissions.allow contains no over-broad patterns (Bash(*), Edit(*), Write(*), match-everything paths) beyond the threshold of 5.
checks.gap: None. All 19 allow entries are scoped to named pnpm scripts, scoped pnpm-exec tool invocations, or read-only git verbs (status, diff, log) plus `git add`. Zero broad patterns found.

checks.expectation: Deployable projects forbid destructive commands in permissions.deny.
checks.gap: None for the baseline (deny is non-empty and names destructive commands: force/any push and recursive delete). Coverage is intentionally minimal — it does not yet cover, e.g., `git reset --hard`, `git clean -fd`, or `git checkout --` mass-discard — but the layer only requires a non-empty deny list naming destructive commands.
checks.remediation: Optional hardening: extend deny with `Bash(git reset --hard:*)`, `Bash(git clean:*)`, and history-rewrite verbs.

checks.expectation: Every hook command invokes a script that exists in the repository.
checks.gap: None. The single PreToolUse(Bash) hook calls .claude/hooks/block-gate-bypass.sh, which exists, is a POSIX sh guard that blocks --no-verify with exit code 2, and matches the rule AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md document.
checks.remediation: No action. The hook only inspects the raw input string for the literal `--no-verify`; it does not normalise quoting/spacing, so a deliberately obfuscated bypass could evade it — acceptable for a guard-rail, not a security boundary.

checks.expectation: No secret-valued env entries; MCP-server entries auditable; statusLine/model overrides intentional and current.
checks.gap: None / not-applicable. No env block, no mcpServers block, no .mcp.json, no statusLine, no model override — nothing to leak and no stale model identifier to flag.

checks.expectation: When multiple instruction files exist, one is the source of truth and the others reference it.
checks.gap: None. CLAUDE.md opens 'Read AGENTS.md first' and declares it adds only the Claude layer. AGENTS.md notes CLAUDE.md sits on top of it. Both defer detail to .agents/, which README.md states is the single source of truth. No conflicting guidance found between the files.
checks.remediation: No action. This is the textbook single-source-of-truth arrangement.

checks.expectation: Frameworks named in instruction files appear in package.json; no stale mentions.
checks.gap: None. React 19, Vite, Jest, RTL, Playwright, Biome, commitlint, husky, Fallow, StyleProof are all present as dependencies/devDependencies. No mention of removed tooling (no Vitest, ESLint, Prettier, Webpack mentions).

checks.expectation: Every script-running command in instruction files references a script that exists in package.json.
checks.gap: None. All documented commands resolve: dev, build, typecheck, check, lint, format, fallow:audit, test, test:watch, test:coverage, e2e, e2e:install, verify, setup:agents. `pnpm install`, `pnpm exec …`, and `pnpm audit` are not-applicable (not custom scripts).

checks.expectation: Internal references in instruction files point at files or directories that exist.
checks.gap: None. Every checked reference resolves on disk: the five .agents/project docs, .agents/decisions/, .agents/templates/ (all four template files), e2e/app.spec.ts, e2e/styleproof.spec.ts, src/features/threshold-counter, README.md, jest.config.ts, biome.json, tsconfig.json, jest.style-stub.cjs, and ADRs 0003/0004.

checks.expectation: Instruction files do not mention retired model IDs.
checks.gap: None. No model identifiers appear anywhere in the agentic corpus, so none are stale.

checks.expectation: In an active project (commits in the last 90 days), at least one instruction file was modified within 180 days.
checks.gap: None. The project is active (latest commit 2026-06-17) and every instruction file was last modified 2026-06-17, well within the freshness window.

checks.expectation: When a 'no abbreviations' policy is declared, the instruction prose itself complies.
checks.gap: CONVENTIONS.md declares a no-abbreviations rule (scoped, by its own wording, to identifiers: 'count not cnt, request not req'). The instruction prose uses the established abbreviations E2E, CI, ADR, UI, and IO. These are widely-accepted domain acronyms rather than the lazy identifier abbreviations the rule targets, so this is a soft partial, not a violation — but a strict reading of a 'no abbreviations' banner invites the question.
checks.remediation: Either (a) scope the rule explicitly to identifiers in CONVENTIONS.md ('No abbreviations in names'), or (b) add a short allowlist of accepted acronyms (E2E, CI, CD, ADR, UI, IO, API). One sentence.
